methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Reflex Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ravi Gondhalekar and Ben Kaplan that has raised $7M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though insufficient narrow-stage peers exist to support reliable peer-median valuation.

Given the limited peer set at Seed stage, the primary applicable method is stage-progression analysis. Using the $7M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in frontier tech, this approach yields an implied valuation of $18M. No peer-median cross-check is feasible; the 11 same-sector comparables lack sufficient density at Seed or adjacent stages to meet statistical thresholds for comparable-company analysis.

The valuation range supported by this single-method triangulation is $15M (low) to $20M (high), with a median point of $18M. This range rests heavily on the stage-progression multiplier assumption. Key uncertainties include the applicability of a 2.5x step-up multiplier to Reflex's specific capital trajectory and product stage, and the absence of peer-based anchoring for calibration. Additional disclosure on traction metrics, cash runway, and round timing would improve confidence in the estimate.
